meta.certificationName: Databricks Certified Generative AI Engineer Associate
meta.title: DBX-GAIA-5: RAG Quality, Governance and Operations

questions.stem: Which pattern BEST describes retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) on Databricks?

options.text: Retrieve relevant context from data, then provide that context to the model during generation
options.explanation: RAG combines retrieval with generation so answers are grounded in enterprise data. It is different from per-prompt fine-tuning or storing embeddings without source context.

options.text: Fine-tune a model on every prompt before answering
Store only embeddings and never the source text
Use batch ETL instead of a model

questions.stem: A team is deciding between fine-tuning a model and using RAG. When is RAG usually the BETTER first choice?

options.text: When the goal is to answer questions over frequently changing enterprise documents
options.explanation: RAG is usually the better first option when knowledge changes often and must stay grounded in current documents. Fine-tuning is not primarily for rapid knowledge refresh.

options.text: When the goal is to teach the model a new tokenizer
When there is no source data to retrieve
When the team wants to remove all prompt construction

questions.stem: Which TWO components are common in a Databricks RAG system? (Select TWO)

options.text: An embedding or retrieval layer for finding relevant passages
options.explanation: RAG commonly uses embeddings/retrieval plus a grounded generation step. It does not require retraining Spark or editing transaction logs.

options.text: A generation step that uses retrieved context
options.explanation: RAG commonly uses embeddings/retrieval plus a grounded generation step. It does not require retraining Spark or editing transaction logs.

options.text: A requirement to retrain Spark itself
A dependency on rewriting Delta Lake transaction logs manually

questions.stem: Users ask short vague questions such as "What changed?" after reading a dashboard. Which improvement is MOST targeted?

options.text: Rewrite the query using conversation context before retrieval
options.explanation: Query rewriting with conversational context is a targeted way to improve vague follow-up questions in RAG systems. The other options reduce grounding or relevance.

options.text: Disable retrieval and answer from the base model only
Increase model temperature to 1.0
Index fewer documents so every query sees the same results

questions.stem: A team wants the chatbot to answer only from approved policy documents. Which architecture is BEST?

options.text: Use a curated corpus, retrieve only from that corpus, and instruct the model to rely on retrieved evidence
options.explanation: Constraining the corpus and grounding on retrieved evidence is the strongest architecture for approved-source answers. The others weaken control.

options.text: Open retrieval to every table in the lakehouse
Skip retrieval because policies are stable
Let users upload arbitrary prompts with no controls

questions.stem: Why do many teams store both source chunks and embeddings?

options.text: Embeddings support retrieval, while source chunks provide readable grounding context
options.explanation: Embeddings help find relevant content, and the original chunks provide the actual context to ground answers. One does not replace the other.

options.text: Embeddings can replace all source data permanently
Source chunks are needed only for billing
Neither is needed once the endpoint is deployed

questions.stem: A prototype answers correctly but often uses too many irrelevant chunks, increasing cost and latency. Which change is MOST appropriate first?

options.text: Tune retrieval settings and context selection before increasing model size
options.explanation: Excess context should be addressed by retrieval tuning and context selection before scaling model size. Larger budgets and higher temperature do not directly solve irrelevant retrieval.

options.text: Double the endpoint budget immediately
Remove metadata filters
Raise temperature to encourage concise answers

questions.stem: A product owner asks for a single metric to judge a RAG assistant. What is the BEST response?

options.text: Use multiple metrics because retrieval quality, faithfulness, and task success capture different failure modes
options.explanation: RAG quality is multi-dimensional. Retrieval relevance, groundedness, and task success each capture different kinds of problems.

options.text: Use token count only
Use endpoint uptime only
Use number of PDFs indexed only

questions.stem: What is a key reason to remove headers, footers, and navigation text before indexing documents?

options.text: They add repetitive noise that can hurt retrieval quality
options.explanation: Boilerplate text often pollutes embeddings and retrieval. Removing it usually improves signal quality.

options.text: They are required by Vector Search
They improve semantic precision automatically
They reduce the number of Delta tables to one

questions.stem: A team wants users to filter search results by region and document type. What must they preserve in the source data?

options.text: Structured metadata columns that can be used at retrieval time
options.explanation: Metadata such as region and document type enables filtered retrieval. Raw bytes or titles alone are not enough for structured filtering.

options.text: Only the raw PDF bytes
Only a document title
Only generated answers

questions.stem: Which TWO chunking practices usually improve RAG quality? (Select TWO)

options.text: Split documents into semantically coherent passages
options.explanation: Coherent chunks and modest overlap help preserve meaning across boundaries. Giant chunks and shuffled sentences usually harm retrieval precision.

options.text: Keep enough overlap to avoid cutting critical context at boundaries
options.explanation: Coherent chunks and modest overlap help preserve meaning across boundaries. Giant chunks and shuffled sentences usually harm retrieval precision.

options.text: Use one giant chunk per document regardless of length
Shuffle sentence order before embedding

questions.stem: A corpus contains many duplicate policy exports with tiny formatting differences. What is the main retrieval risk?

options.text: Top results may be crowded by near-duplicates instead of diverse useful evidence
options.explanation: Near-duplicates can dominate nearest-neighbor results and reduce context diversity. The other platform features are unaffected.

options.text: The model endpoint cannot start
Unity Catalog stops tracking lineage
Delta tables lose ACID guarantees

questions.stem: Why is preserving source lineage valuable for GenAI applications?

options.text: It helps reviewers trace answers back to the original source documents
options.explanation: Lineage improves trust, debugging, and governance by making answer sources traceable. It does not replace testing, monitoring, or good chunking.

options.text: It removes the need for prompt testing
It replaces endpoint monitoring
It guarantees perfect chunking

questions.stem: Which source format is usually easiest to ground answers from after preprocessing?

options.text: Cleaned text chunks with useful metadata
options.explanation: Cleaned text chunks with metadata are straightforward to embed, filter, and cite. The alternatives reduce retrievability.

options.text: Screenshots of every page only
Compressed archives with no extracted text
Randomly sampled sentences

questions.stem: A team indexes source text that includes stale content and current content with the same title. What is the BEST mitigation?

options.text: Track freshness metadata and prefer current authoritative documents during retrieval
options.explanation: Freshness metadata helps retrieval prefer current authoritative content. The other changes do not solve stale-source confusion.

options.text: Increase model temperature
Disable evaluations
Store only embeddings with no document IDs

questions.stem: When building a multilingual assistant, which preprocessing choice matters MOST?

options.text: Use a consistent strategy for language handling across documents and queries
options.explanation: A multilingual pipeline needs a deliberate language strategy so retrieval and prompting stay aligned. The other options degrade quality.

options.text: Convert every document to uppercase
Delete all metadata to simplify vectors
Disable vector search and use exact string matching only

questions.stem: What does Databricks Vector Search provide?

options.text: Managed vector indexing and similarity retrieval over data in the lakehouse
options.explanation: Databricks Vector Search provides managed vector retrieval capabilities. It does not replace permissions, act only as a scheduler, or serve as an offline editor.

options.text: A replacement for Unity Catalog permissions
A scheduler for Spark jobs only
A tool for editing notebooks offline

questions.stem: A team needs a vector index that stays synchronized with a Delta table as source rows change. Which index style is MOST appropriate?

options.text: A delta-sync index
options.explanation: Delta-sync indexes are designed to stay synchronized with Delta Lake source data. CSV exports and temporary views do not provide managed synchronization.

options.text: A CSV export index
A notebook-only cache
A temporary SQL view

questions.stem: Which TWO capabilities are common production needs for a model serving endpoint? (Select TWO)

options.text: Monitoring request latency and error rates
options.explanation: Production endpoints need observability and access control. Manual log editing and removing evaluations are not desirable endpoint capabilities.

options.text: Controlling access and usage to the endpoint
options.explanation: Production endpoints need observability and access control. Manual log editing and removing evaluations are not desirable endpoint capabilities.

options.text: Editing Delta transaction logs manually
Replacing all evaluation datasets

questions.stem: A team wants to call external commercial foundation models through Databricks while applying centralized controls. Which feature is MOST relevant?

options.text: AI Gateway or managed serving controls around model access and usage
options.explanation: Centralized serving controls such as AI Gateway-related capabilities are the relevant mechanism for governing external model usage. The other options address different platform concerns.

options.text: DBFS root mounts
Photon execution only
Delta sharing recipients

questions.stem: Why would a team register prompts, evaluation assets, or models with governed workflows?

options.text: To improve reproducibility and lifecycle management across experiments and deployments
options.explanation: Governed lifecycle management improves reproducibility and deployment discipline. It does not eliminate data needs, CI/CD, or latency concerns.

options.text: To avoid storing source data forever
To remove the need for CI/CD
To guarantee zero latency

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of a serving endpoint in a GenAI app?

options.text: Expose a model or agent for inference requests
options.explanation: Serving endpoints expose models or agents so applications can send inference requests. The other tasks are separate platform concerns.

options.text: Create Delta tables
Ingest PDFs from scanners only
Schedule warehouse maintenance

questions.stem: A developer wants structured JSON output for downstream automation. Which approach is BEST?

options.text: Constrain the prompt or response format so the model returns predictable structured output
options.explanation: Structured-output prompting or response constraints are the right approach for downstream automation. Creative free-form text and random retries are brittle.

options.text: Ask for a creative essay and parse it later with regex
Disable response validation
Rely on random sampling until JSON appears

questions.stem: A retrieval service works, but users experience high latency spikes during traffic bursts. Which first action is MOST reasonable?

options.text: Inspect endpoint and retrieval latency metrics to locate the bottleneck before changing architecture
options.explanation: Latency spikes should be diagnosed with observability data before major architectural changes. Deleting the index or removing monitoring is counterproductive.

options.text: Delete the vector index
Switch to a larger context window without measurement
Remove all monitoring to reduce overhead

questions.stem: Why should teams minimize sensitive information in prompts?

options.text: Prompts can expose confidential data to downstream model interactions and logs
options.explanation: Prompt minimization helps reduce unnecessary exposure of sensitive data. It does not change dimensions, guarantee lower cost, or prevent all hallucinations.

options.text: It increases embedding dimensionality
It guarantees cheaper tokens
It prevents all hallucinations

questions.stem: A company wants only approved teams to access a GenAI application and its underlying data. Which principle should guide the design?

options.text: Least privilege across both application access and underlying data access
options.explanation: Least privilege should apply to both the app and the retrieved data. Broad access and shared admin accounts increase risk.

options.text: Give every employee broad read access for convenience
Store credentials in notebook comments
Use a single shared admin account

questions.stem: Which TWO practices help reduce harmful outputs before production release? (Select TWO)

options.text: Test with adversarial and edge-case prompts
options.explanation: Adversarial testing and explicit policy constraints both reduce production risk. More randomness without testing and skipping review increase risk.

options.text: Add policy constraints and grounding instructions to the system behavior
options.explanation: Adversarial testing and explicit policy constraints both reduce production risk. More randomness without testing and skipping review increase risk.

options.text: Increase randomness without evaluation
Skip human review for high-risk use cases

questions.stem: A compliance assistant must not answer beyond approved documents. What is the strongest control?

options.text: Ground the answer on retrieved approved context and evaluate unsupported-claim failures explicitly
options.explanation: Grounding plus targeted evaluation of unsupported claims is stronger than generic caution prompts. Token limits and disabled feedback do not enforce factual scope.

options.text: Ask the base model to be careful
Increase token limits
Disable feedback collection

questions.stem: Why is human review still important for some GenAI workflows?

options.text: High-impact decisions may need oversight even when automated quality looks good
options.explanation: High-impact workflows often need human oversight. Human review complements, not replaces, testing and access controls.

options.text: It replaces all automated testing permanently
It is required for every low-risk autocomplete
It eliminates the need for access controls

questions.stem: Which statement about hallucinations is MOST accurate?

options.text: Good retrieval and evaluation can reduce them, but they cannot be assumed away
options.explanation: Hallucinations can be reduced with grounding and evaluation but not assumed impossible. Endpoint size and prompt length alone do not eliminate them.

options.text: They disappear automatically on larger endpoints
They occur only when prompts are too short
They are unrelated to grounding

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of offline evaluation datasets for GenAI apps?

options.text: To compare changes before they affect production users
options.explanation: Offline evaluation datasets let teams compare versions safely before rollout. They do not replace monitoring or documentation.

options.text: To replace all production monitoring
To avoid documenting prompts
To eliminate source data requirements

questions.stem: A team notices that answer quality drops after new documents are added. Which check is MOST appropriate first?

options.text: Verify ingestion, chunking, and retrieval quality for the newly added content
options.explanation: A quality drop after ingesting new content usually points first to ingestion, chunking, or retrieval issues. The other steps ignore likely root causes.

options.text: Increase temperature to encourage broader answers
Delete historical evaluation results
Stop tracking latency

questions.stem: Which TWO operational metrics are especially useful for a production GenAI service? (Select TWO)

options.text: Latency percentiles
options.explanation: Latency and usage are core operational signals for cost and user experience. Color themes and emojis are not meaningful service metrics.

options.text: Token or request usage trends
options.explanation: Latency and usage are core operational signals for cost and user experience. Color themes and emojis are not meaningful service metrics.

options.text: Notebook revision color themes
Number of emojis in prompts

questions.stem: A system passes offline tests but fails for a new class of user requests in production. What is the BEST response?

options.text: Add those production failure cases to the evaluation set and iterate on retrieval or prompts
options.explanation: New production failure modes should be folded back into evaluation and used to improve the system. Ignoring them or removing logs makes maintenance worse.

options.text: Ignore them because offline tests already passed
Disable logging so users stop filing examples
Increase model randomness

questions.stem: Why is prompt versioning useful in a GenAI application lifecycle?

options.text: It lets teams compare behavior across revisions and roll back if needed
options.explanation: Prompt versioning supports controlled iteration and rollback. It does not guarantee perfection or replace serving and feedback loops.

options.text: It prevents every possible failure automatically
It replaces model serving endpoints
It removes the need for user feedback

questions.stem: A team wants to know whether retrieval changes or prompt changes caused a quality regression. Which practice helps MOST?

options.text: Change one major variable at a time and record evaluation results for each revision
options.explanation: Controlled experimentation and recorded evaluations are the clearest way to isolate regressions. Changing everything together makes root cause analysis harder.

options.text: Change everything together for speed
Stop measuring grounding
Use only anecdotal user comments

questions.stem: What is the value of collecting user feedback on GenAI answers?

options.text: It helps uncover real failure modes that may not appear in curated tests
options.explanation: User feedback exposes realistic edge cases and complements offline tests. It does not replace source data, latency monitoring, or deliberate prompt tuning.

options.text: It eliminates the need for source documents
It makes latency irrelevant
It automatically tunes all prompts

questions.stem: An endpoint is healthy, but costs spike sharply after a feature launch. Which investigation is MOST direct?

options.text: Check request volume, prompt length, retrieved context size, and token usage after the launch
options.explanation: Cost spikes often come from more traffic, larger prompts, bigger retrieved contexts, or higher token usage. Those are the first operational factors to inspect.

options.text: Delete the evaluation set
Turn off access controls
Reduce notebook permissions only

questions.stem: Why should production monitoring include both quality and operational signals?

options.text: A service can be fast but wrong, or accurate but unstable; both matter
options.explanation: Production success depends on both answer quality and system reliability/cost. Either category alone is incomplete.

options.text: Operational signals are enough on their own
Quality signals are enough on their own
Neither matters after initial launch

questions.stem: What is a strong release practice for a materially changed GenAI workflow?

options.text: Evaluate it against representative tasks before broad rollout and keep rollback options ready
options.explanation: Representative evaluation plus rollback readiness is a mature release practice. Immediate global rollout and reduced governance are risky.

options.text: Deploy it globally without checks because the old version worked
Skip governance reviews if latency improves
Disable logs to save storage
